description.cs: Profil entity. Umožňuje blíže specifikovat některé její vlastnost v kontextu aplikačního profilu.
description.en: Entity profile. Allows profile editors to specify context dependent information such as label, definition, usage note, cardinalities, etc.
Profile of a class. Allows specification editors to specify profile specific names, definitions and usage notes.
Profile of a property allows specification editors to specify profile specific name, definition, usage note, domain, range and cardinality.

Specifies, which values of which properties of which resources are reused for the current Profile.
E.g. reuse of profile name, reuse of class name, reuse of profile definition, reuse of profile usage note, etc.
The class resource, everything.

usageNote.en: In the context of DSV, Term is used as a common denominator for Classes, Properties and their profiles.

description.en: The class of RDF properties.
The class of classes.

description.cs: Neplatný profil je profil, který porušuje pravidla pro správné profilování. To může být z dobrého důvodu, jako je dočasná nekonzistence, která se má teprve projevit na profilované entitě.
description.en: Invalid profile is a profile, which breaks profile compliance rules. This may be for a good reason such as temporary, to be propagated upstream, inconsistency.
The defininition of the subject resource.
The property profiled by the property profile.
The class of RDF datatypes.
Datatype used with this Datatype property profile.
Class profile that is the range of the object property profile.
The Resource on which there is a property with a value for reuse.
Points to definitions of Property value reuse for this Profile.
A related resource in which the described resource is physically or logically included.
Specifies a profile specialized by this profile.
The profiled class.
Specifies a parent profile of the current profile.
Class role, e.g. Mandatory, Supportive, etc.
Requirement level of the property, typically mandatory, recommended or optional.
Link to an external documentation of this resource. Profiles of this resource should preferably link to this URL for documentation.
Preferred label of this profile.
Definition of this profile.
Usage note of this profile.
Vocabulary contains definitions of classes, properties and their relations, possibly to classes and properties from other vocabularies.

description.cs: Konceptuální model obsahující profily tříd a vlastností různých slovníků.
description.en: An Application Profile identifies classes and properties from vocabularies, and class profiles and property profiles from Application profiles that are re-used in a certain usage context. It also allows specification editors to further adjust the description of re-used classes and properties for that usage context without the need to explicitly create subclasses and subproperties.
An Application Profile Specification contains resources defining and describing an Application Profile, including the human readable documentation, machine readable definition, etc.
A Vocabulary Specification consists of resources defining and describing a Vocabulary, e.g. definitions of classes and properties, typically with their future re-use in application profiles in mind.
A human-readable Data Specification Document.
A name given to the Data Specification Document.
An account of the Data Specification Document.
A name given to the Semantic Data Specification.
An account of the Semantic Data Specification.
The URL of a downloadable file with particulars such as its format and role indicated by the Resource Descriptor
The preferred identifier for the Profile, for use in circumstances where its URI cannot be used

usageNote.en: Used to store the preferred prefix, e.g., registered at prefix.cc.

description.en: A role that an profile resource, described by a Resource Descriptor, plays
The function of an artifact described by a Resource Descriptor, such as specification, guidance etc.
Class Role, e.g. Mandatory or Supportive.
Requirement Level of a property profile.
Cardinality, e.g. 0..* or 1..1
An idea or notion; a unit of thought.

usageNote.en: The file format, physical medium, or dimensions of the resource.

description.en: The file format, physical medium, or dimensions of the resource.
An established standard to which the described resource conforms.
The class of object properties.
The class of data properties.
A human-readable name for the subject.
A definition of the subject resource.
The subject is a subclass of a class.
The subject is a subproperty of a property.
A domain of the subject property.
A range of the subject property.
A range of the subject property.
The Property to gain the reused value.

usageNote.en: This is expected to be one of skos:prefLabel for preferred label, skos:definition for definition, or skos:scopeNote for usage note.
